name: Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo
name_original: Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Ermita de Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monasterio de Irache – vuelta desde Acedo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mendaza feature varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. The region offers a network of roads with notable elevation changes, as indicated by routes with over 1,000 meters of ascent. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, providing options for both challenging climbs and more moderate rides. Best road cycling routes around Mendaza The most popular road cycling route is Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo, a 70.1 miles (112.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This route features substantial climbs and descents through the local landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de La Aldea – Puerto de Meano loop from Ancín / Antzin, a 42.7 miles (68.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Mendaza is defined by rolling hills, open countryside, and challenging ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Mendaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mendaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mendaza?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Mendaza, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mendaza?
A: The region generally offers favorable conditions for cycling during summer, with spring and fall also being ideal for pleasant temperatures and scenic rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Mendaza?
A: Road cycling in Mendaza is characterized by rolling hills, open countryside, and challenging ascents. You'll find routes with significant elevation changes, suitable for those seeking both moderate and difficult rides.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Mendaza?
A: Yes, Mendaza offers a few easier road cycling routes. While many routes feature substantial climbs, there are options available for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Mendaza?
A: The road cycling routes in Mendaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to challenging ascents, and the scenic open countryside.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Mendaza?
A: Yes, many routes in Mendaza are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986763909/bernedo-and-puerto-de-la-aldea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bernedo and Puerto de la Aldea Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31872600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Aldea – Puerto de Meano loop from Ancín / Antzin&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Mendaza?
A: As you cycle around Mendaza, you can encounter several natural landmarks. Notable mountain peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3431427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costalera&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7580262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Summit (1,338 m)&lt;/a&gt;. You might also find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4108046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Mendaza is known for its challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984876927/ibernalo-iturrieta-hermitage-of-santa-teodesia-estella-monastery-of-irache-return-from-acedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, features over 1,480 meters of ascent over 112 km, making it a demanding ride.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Mendaza?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Shorter, moderate routes might take around 3 hours, while longer, more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984876927/ibernalo-iturrieta-hermitage-of-santa-teodesia-estella-monastery-of-irache-return-from-acedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibernalo - Iturrieta - Hermitage of Santa Teodesia - Estella - Monastery of Irache – return from Acedo&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic views in Mendaza?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Mendaza offer picturesque views of the surrounding landscape, including open countryside and mountain vistas. The region's diverse terrain provides ample opportunities for scenic rides.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes in Mendaza?
A: Yes, out of the 37 available routes, 15 are classified as moderate difficulty. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marañón Fountain – Puerto De Arenaza Sabando loop from Zúñiga&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a balanced challenge without the extreme demands of the most difficult trails.
tips: Place of worship and recreation near Santa Cruz de Campezo. The rural house that shares a building has a restaurant with some skewers that are worth it
The climb up to the hermitage is quite a demanding challenge with sections of slope above 10% and some 15% ramp but you can be sure that the views when you get here are worth every drop of sweat!

Without a doubt, the most magical place in Estella. The Plaza de San Martín brings together several of the most emblematic buildings such as the Palace of the Kings of Navarra, the old town hall and, in the background, the Church of San Pedro de la Rúa. The best of all is to see how the mountain almost enters the very square and becomes part of urban life. In the square there are several bars and restaurants and a fountain.

The Monastery of Irache (in Basque: Iratxeko monasterioa, the spelling Iratxe is most commonly used in both languages) is a monastic complex located in the Navarrese town of Ayegui in Spain. Its origins date back to the eighth century and some of the current buildings keep parts from the eleventh century. It was run by the Benedictines.

It is one of the most important monumental complexes of the autonomous community of Navarre in which the Romanesque church of the twelfth century, the Plateresque cloister and the Herrerian tower stand out. It is located on the side of the Camino de Santiago and near Estella.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monasterio_de_Santa_Mar%C3%ADa_la_Real_de_Irache

The fountain has two pipes, one with water and one with wine. It is the only source of wine that exists along the route of the Camino. Every day the fountain is filled with about 100 liters of young red wine, to quench the pilgrim's thirst and make his way more bearable.
https://www.irache.com/es/fuente-del-vino.html
Villamayor de Monjardin is a wine village with the ruins of the Roman fortress San Esteban above it.

waypoints.name: Views of the vineyards of Villamayor de Monjardín
